title: Incorrect evaluation of combinational circuit with const-bit-op-tree
body:
I have a simple combinational circuit (generated by yosys), where one bit is evaluated incorrectly if verilator optimisation is turned on. I am attaching a SystemVerilog file and a minimal C++ testbench showing the problem.

```
>> lsb_release -d
Description:	Ubuntu 22.10

>> /usr/local/bin/verilator --version
Verilator 5.009 devel rev v5.008-81-g277bd67f7

>> /usr/local/bin/verilator -Wall -O3 --trace --trace-underscore --prefix DUT --top-module mymodule mymodule.sv --cc --exe --build testbench.cpp && ./obj_dir/DUT
[...]
Expected  0xc4
Got       0xc5
```

The problem apparently arises in this line of the SystemVerilog file:
```
assign result_o[0] = ~(_006_ ^ _013_);
```
Looking at the generated trace file it can be seen that signal `_006_` is 0 and signal `_013_` is 1, therefore `result_o[0]` should be 0, but it is instead 1. If optimisation flag `-O3` is replaced by `-O0` the result is calculated correctly.
[module_and_testbench.tar.gz](https://github.com/verilator/verilator/files/11039773/module_and_testbench.tar.gz)
